data.title: U.S. SEC Chair Comments on Potential Seizure of Venezuela's Alleged Bitcoin Holdings
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on January 13, U.S. SEC Chair Paul Atkins stated that the decision on whether the United States will seize Venezuela's alleged Bitcoin assets remains uncertain. In an interview with Fox Business, Atkins addressed claims that Venezuela might possess up to $6 billion worth of Bitcoin, approximately 60,000 BTC. He noted that several blockchain analysts have been unable to verify these claims. The decision regarding the disposition of these assets will be made by other government departments, as the SEC is not involved in this matter.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams Affirms Feasibility of 2% Inflation Target
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve official Williams has stated that restoring the inflation rate to the 2% target is 'completely realistic.'

data.title: SEC Chairman: Whether the US will confiscate Venezuelan-held Bitcoin remains to be seen.
data.description: In an interview with Fox Business, U.S. SEC Chairman Paul Atkins stated that it remains to be seen whether U.S. authorities will confiscate the alleged Bitcoin held by Venezuela following the arrest of the Venezuelan president. Regarding reports that Venezuela holds $60 billion worth of Bitcoin (approximately 600,000 BTC), Atkins stated that Venezuela is not involved in such decisions and that they will be handled by other government departments. Several blockchain analytics firms have not yet confirmed the claim that the country holds $60 billion worth of cryptocurrency. Furthermore, the U.S. Senate Banking Committee plans to consider the Digital Asset Market Transparency Act (CLARITY) this Thursday. This bill aims to grant the Commodity Futures Trading Commission more regulatory power over digital assets, but may be delayed due to factors such as the 2026 midterm elections.

data.title: Bitmine increased its pledged holdings by 154,000 ETH today, bringing the total pledged ETH to $4.15 billion.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, Bitmine (@BitMNR) has staked an additional 154,208 ETH, worth $478.77 million. Currently, Bitmine has staked a total of 1,344,224 ETH, with a total value of $4.15 billion.

data.title: U.S. Senate Agriculture Committee Delays Crypto Market Structure Bill Revision
data.description: According to PANews, U.S. Senate Agriculture Committee Chairman John Boozman announced a delay in the committee's revision of the cryptocurrency market structure bill. Initially scheduled to coincide with the Senate Banking Committee's session on Thursday, the revision will now take place in the last week of January. Boozman stated that additional time is necessary to maintain bipartisan support.

data.title: U.S. Economic Growth Forecasted to Reach 2.5% to 2.75% by 2026
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve official Williams has indicated that the United States' economic growth rate could range between 2.5% and 2.75% by 2026.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams Optimistic About 2026 Economic Outlook
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve's Williams has expressed optimism about the economic outlook for 2026, describing it as "quite favorable." He also noted that current monetary policy is closer to a neutral stance.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Williams Emphasizes Inflation Control Without Job Market Risks
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve's Williams stated that the Federal Reserve must reduce inflation without posing undue risks to the job market.

data.title: Meta Platforms Considers Layoffs in Reality Labs Division
data.description: According to Odaily, Meta Platforms is contemplating layoffs within its Reality Labs division, specifically targeting the branch involved in 'metaverse' operations. Three informed employees revealed that the company might initiate these layoffs as early as next month, potentially affecting 10% to 30% of the team. This team primarily focuses on VR headsets and VR-based social networks. Reality Labs comprises the metaverse and wearable devices departments. Sources indicate that management plans to redirect the savings from these layoffs towards the AR glasses project. Meta launched AR glasses in collaboration with Ray-Ban in 2021, surpassing internal sales targets. Insiders report that Meta is reassessing its investments in the metaverse, with executives considering significant cuts to VR-related positions within the metaverse department.
